== New Settings in Trunk (Not in cassandra-5.0)

These settings appear as top-level `== \`setting_name\`` sections in the trunk-generated output but not in the 5.0 output.

=== Accord / Consensus
* `accord` ‚Äî new TCM/Accord configuration block (complex option)

=== Address Configuration
* `addresses_config` ‚Äî new addresses configuration block
* `initial_location_provider` ‚Äî provider for node initial location
* `node_proximity` ‚Äî node proximity configuration
* `prefer_local_connections` ‚Äî prefer local connections setting

=== Auto Repair
* `auto_repair` ‚Äî new auto repair configuration block

=== Bulk Load
* `bulk_load_enabled` ‚Äî controls whether bulk loading is enabled

=== Collection Size Guard Thresholds
* `collection_list_size_fail_threshold`
* `collection_list_size_warn_threshold`
* `collection_map_size_fail_threshold`
* `collection_map_size_warn_threshold`
* `collection_set_size_fail_threshold`
* `collection_set_size_warn_threshold`

=== Column Value Size Guard Thresholds
* `column_ascii_value_size_fail_threshold`
* `column_ascii_value_size_warn_threshold`
* `column_blob_value_size_fail_threshold`
* `column_blob_value_size_warn_threshold`
* `column_text_and_varchar_value_size_fail_threshold`
* `column_text_and_varchar_value_size_warn_threshold`

=== Compaction
* `compaction_read_disk_access_mode` ‚Äî disk access mode for compaction reads

=== Compression Dictionary Cache
* `compression_dictionary_cache_expire` ‚Äî expiry for cached compression dictionaries
* `compression_dictionary_cache_size` ‚Äî max dictionaries cached per table
* `compression_dictionary_refresh_initial_delay` ‚Äî initial delay before first refresh
* `compression_dictionary_refresh_interval` ‚Äî interval between cache refreshes

=== Disk Usage Protection
* `data_disk_usage_keyspace_wide_protection_enabled` ‚Äî enables keyspace-wide disk usage protection

=== GC Logging Thresholds
* `gc_concurrent_phase_log_threshold`
* `gc_concurrent_phase_warn_threshold`

=== JMX
* `jmx_server_options` ‚Äî JMX server options (complex option; new in trunk)

=== Keyspace Properties Guardrails
* `keyspace_properties_disallowed`
* `keyspace_properties_ignored`
* `keyspace_properties_warned`

=== Materialized Views
* `materialized_views_on_repair_enabled` ‚Äî whether MVs participate in repair

=== Maximum Length Guardrails
* `max_comment_length` ‚Äî maximum allowed comment length
* `max_security_label_length` ‚Äî maximum allowed security label length

=== Password Policy
* `password_policy` ‚Äî password policy configuration block (complex option)
* `password_policy_reconfiguration_enabled` ‚Äî enables runtime password policy reconfiguration

=== Repair Disk Headroom
* `incremental_repair_disk_headroom_reject_ratio`
* `reject_repair_compaction_threshold`
* `repair_disk_headroom_reject_ratio`

=== Role Name Policy
* `role_name_policy` ‚Äî role name policy configuration block (complex option)
* `role_name_policy_reconfiguration_enabled` ‚Äî enables runtime role name policy reconfiguration

=== SSTable Read Logging
* `sstables_per_read_log_threshold`

=== Tombstone Thresholds
* `tombstone_read_purgeable_metric_granularity`
* `tombstone_warn_threshold` ‚Äî note: this setting existed in 5.0; its section position changed in trunk YAML, producing a diff artifact

=== Triggers
* `triggers_policy` ‚Äî policy controlling trigger execution

=== Unset Training
* `unset_training_min_frequency_enabled`
* `unset_training_min_frequency_warned`

== Settings Removed in Trunk vs cassandra-5.0

* `native_transport_port_ssl` ‚Äî SSL-specific native transport port setting; removed from trunk YAML (functionality may be covered by `native_transport_tls_port` or unified TLS config ‚Äî verify against trunk source before documenting as removed)

== Notes on Diff Methodology

* The 5.0 YAML was retrieved via: `git show origin/cassandra-5.0:conf/cassandra.yaml`
* The trunk YAML is the working-tree file at `conf/cassandra.yaml` on the `trunk` branch
* Both were processed with the same script: `doc/scripts/convert_yaml_to_adoc.py`
* The diff was computed as: `diff <5.0-output> <trunk-output>`
* `tombstone_warn_threshold` appears as both added and removed because it is present at two distinct positions in the trunk YAML, producing two `==` sections, while 5.0 had only one ‚Äî this is a quirk of the source YAML, not a real removal
* The full raw diff (962 lines) is available at: `/tmp/yaml_adoc_5.0_vs_trunk.diff` (not committed; re-generate as needed)
